macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Sony’s Project Ace robot beats elite human table tennis player
items.summary: Sony’s Project Ace reportedly created the first robot to defeat an elite human table tennis player, using nine cameras to analyze spin and track the ball in real time. The demo highlights rapid advances in robotic perception, control, and reinforcement learning.

items.title: Anthropic’s secret “Mythos” AI model allegedly triggers banking security concerns
items.summary: A video report claims Anthropic has a specialist security model called Mythos that found a 27-year-old OpenBSD bug and produced a working remote code exploit overnight. It also alleges the model prompted emergency discussions among central bankers and partner organizations, underscoring rising concern about offensive AI capabilities.

items.title: AI news roundup spotlights Claude for SMBs, Codex on mobile, and Google Workspace integration
items.summary: A weekly AI news roundup highlights several company updates, including Anthropic’s Claude for Small Business, Anthropic’s Claude for Legal, OpenAI’s Codex integration into the ChatGPT mobile app, and Google Workspace Studio connecting with NotebookLM. The roundup also points to broader competition among major AI vendors over business adoption and productivity workflows.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Tomodachi Life tops Japan’s weekly game sales for fourth straight week
items.summary: Nintendo’s Tomodachi Life remained No. 1 in Japan for the week of May 4-10, selling 149,245 copies and passing 1.04 million lifetime units. The chart also shows strong continued sales for Pokémon Pokopia and Mario Kart World.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Apple's revamped Siri may launch in beta with auto-delete chat option
items.summary: Apple is reportedly preparing a revamped Siri for beta release, adding an option for chats to auto-delete. The feature would give Apple’s chatbot a privacy angle that could set it apart from other assistant products.

items.title: Meta to bring third-party apps and games to its display glasses
items.summary: Meta is expanding the usefulness of its $800 display glasses by adding support for third-party apps and games. The move could make the glasses a more compelling consumer device beyond core camera and display features.

items.title: SpaceX reportedly getting ready to go public as early as June
items.summary: SpaceX is reportedly preparing for a potential public offering as soon as June. Reuters says the company could make the initial announcement by next Wednesday, a major step for one of the world's most valuable private companies.

items.title: Apple reportedly testing Intel's 18A-P process for iPhone and Mac chips
items.summary: Apple is said to be evaluating Intel's 18A-P manufacturing process for future iPhone and Mac chips. The initial testing run would reportedly focus on older Apple Silicon systems.

items.title: ASML partners with Tata Electronics on India's $11B chip factory
items.summary: ASML has entered a partnership with Tata Electronics to help bring a large chip factory online in Gujarat. The deal supports India's push to expand domestic semiconductor manufacturing capacity.
